headline: Core Lido V3 contracts (stETH proxy+impl, Locator) verified on Etherscan; public source in lidofinance/core; recent audits by Certora, Consensys Diligence, MixBytes cover deployed version.

findings.text: Lido/stETH proxy (0xae7ab96520DE3A18E5e111B5EaAb095312D7fE84) verified on Etherscan (AppProxyUpgradeable, Similar Match); impl (0x6ca84080381E43938476814be61B779A8bB6a600) verified as Lido contract. Locator proxy (0xC1d0b3DE6792Bf6b4b37EccdcC24e45978Cfd2Eb) verified (OssifiableProxy); impl (0x2f8779042EFaEd4c53db2Ce293eB6B3f7096C72d) verified as LidoLocator. Standard proxy patterns accepted per checklist.

Canonical public repo https://github.com/lidofinance/core (default master branch, active V3 development) contains contract sources (Lido.sol, LidoLocator.sol etc.) whose structure and contents correspond to the verified Etherscan source for deployed V3.0.1 mainnet contracts; releases and deployed-mainnet.json artifacts align with docs.lido.fi listings.

https://github.com/lidofinance/audits lists multiple Lido V3 audits: Certora Lido V3 (12-2025 formal + 03-2026 fix review), Consensys Diligence Lido V3 (11-2025), MixBytes Lido v3 (03-2026), Composable Security Oracle V7 (2026), explicitly scoping core contracts including Lido V3 components.

Auditors explicitly named and recognized per checklist: Certora (formal verification), Consensys Diligence, MixBytes, Ackee Blockchain, Sigma Prime — all appear in the recognized Solidity auditor list.

Most recent audits (MixBytes Lido v3 03-2026, Certora fix review 03-2026) align with or post-date Lido V3.0.1 mainnet deployment (per docs.lido.fi and core/releases/tag/v3.0.1); fix reviews indicate post-audit changes were re-reviewed with no flagged un-audited drift in core logic.
Assessed proxies have separately verified implementations on Etherscan (Lido impl and Locator impl both confirmed verified); no proxy-with-unverified-impl pattern observed for primary contracts.

steelman.red: The primary stETH contract still uses a legacy 0.4.24 AppProxyUpgradeable and older Solidity version potentially predating full V3 audit scope, with only similar-match verification and no byte-level reproduction performed, creating a plausible verifiability gap for the core entrypoint.
steelman.orange: While proxies and impls are explorer-verified and multiple recognized-firm audits exist for V3, the exact deployed bytecode-to-repo commit match was not independently confirmed via diff or pinned artifact SHA, and secondary contracts (e.g. StakingRouter, AccountingOracle impls) were not individually re-checked, leaving minor residual uncertainty on full coverage.
steelman.green: Every inspected core contract (Lido proxy+impl, Locator proxy+impl) is fully verified on Etherscan with standard proxy patterns and matching public source from the official lidofinance/core repo at the live V3.0.1 version; recent comprehensive audits (including formal verification and fix reviews) from top recognized firms explicitly cover the deployed contracts with no material post-audit drift, satisfying all green criteria.

rationale.verdict: Choosing green because the direct Etherscan verifications of the primary user-facing contracts (stETH entrypoint and Locator), combined with the official public repo correspondence and multiple recent recognized-firm audits (Certora formal + fix review, Consensys Diligence, MixBytes) on the exact live V3 deployment, provide stronger positive evidence than the scope-limited gaps (no bytecode diff) cited in the orange steelman; red arguments overstate legacy elements that remain fully verified and re-audited.

evidence.shows: Lists all Ethereum mainnet production addresses for Lido V3.0.1 including Lido/stETH proxy 0xae7ab96520DE3A18E5e111B5EaAb095312D7fE84 + impl 0x6ca84080381E43938476814be61B779A8bB6a600, Locator proxy 0xC1d0b3DE6792Bf6b4b37EccdcC24e45978Cfd2Eb + impl 0x2f8779042EFaEd4c53db2Ce293eB6B3f7096C72d, and other core proxies/impls; confirms active V3 deployment status.
Lido/stETH proxy contract source verified (Similar Match, AppProxyUpgradeable, Solidity 0.4.24); explicitly identifies implementation address 0x6ca84080381E43938476814be61B779A8bB6a600.
Lido implementation contract source verified (Similar Match, contract name Lido, Solidity 0.4.24); source code available.
Lido Locator proxy verified (OssifiableProxy / ERC1967-based, Solidity 0.8.9); identifies implementation 0x2f8779042EFaEd4c53db2Ce293eB6B3f7096C72d.
LidoLocator implementation verified (Similar Match, Solidity 0.8.9, contract name LidoLocator); full source available.

Repository of Lido audits including Certora Lido V3 Audit Report (12-2025) + fix review (03-2026), Consensys Diligence Lido V3 Security Audit (11-2025), MixBytes Lido v3 Security Audit Report (03-2026), and others scoping core V3 contracts; direct PDF links provided.

Canonical source repository for Lido DAO smart contracts and V3 (master branch, releases v3.0.1/v3.0.2, deployed-mainnet.json artifacts, links to audits); contract sources correspond to Etherscan-verified deployed code.
Primary public governance forum (Discourse) for Lido DAO proposals, discussions, and community input; linked from lido.fi/governance.
Official Lido bug bounty program on Immunefi covering smart contracts and applications (up to $2M rewards); distinct from Safe Harbor.
LDO ERC-20 governance voting token contract on Ethereum (verified source).

unknowns: V2: exact commit SHA or bytecode diff confirming 1:1 match between deployed mainnet bytecode and a specific repo commit not performed (scope limit; version tag v3.0.1 alignment used instead)

protocol_metadata.about: Lido is Ethereum's largest liquid staking protocol: users deposit any amount of ETH to mint stETH (a transferable, reward-accruing receipt token usable across DeFi) while the protocol routes stake to a permissionless set of node operators. Lido V3 adds stVaults for customizable validator setups with optional liquidity and external shares, alongside the Community Staking Module. Governance occurs via LDO token holders using off-chain Snapshot votes and on-chain execution; the system is distinguished by its scale, on-chain accounting transparency, extensive formal + manual audits, and progressive decentralization.
